text: The surgeon uses a da Vinci robotic system to perform a hysterectomy, manipulating the round ligament on the right side with robotic scissors. The bipolar Maryland instrument is visible on the left side. The anterior leaf of the broad ligament is opened, revealing underlying structures.

The surgeon uses monopolar scissors to separate the UV fold and the bladder. The right uterine artery is coagulated and cut. On the left side, the round ligament, fallopian tube, and ovarian ligament are sequentially separated. The posterior peritoneum is dissected up to the cervix at the junction with the uterosacral ligament. The left uterine artery is coagulated medially and then cut.
The surgeon uses a full circle colpotomizer to perform a colpotomy at the tip of the device. The anterior colpotomy is completed, and the posterior colpotomy is currently being carried out.
The uterus is extracted through the vaginal opening and temporarily placed there to prevent gas leakage. The surgeon uses a bidirectional barbed suture to close the vaginal vault.
